quizQuestion: Who directed The Godfather (1972)?
explanation: Francis Ford Coppola directed The Godfather (1972), co-writing the screenplay with novelist Mario Puzo. The film launched one of cinema's most celebrated trilogies.
statement: The Godfather was directed by Francis Ford Coppola.
wowFactor: Coppola nearly got fired during production — the studio wanted him replaced before he delivered one of cinema's greatest films.

quizQuestion: Who played Vito Corleone in The Godfather?
explanation: Marlon Brando portrayed Vito Corleone in The Godfather (1972), stuffing his cheeks with cotton to create the character's distinctive gravelly voice and jowled appearance.
statement: Marlon Brando played patriarch Vito Corleone in The Godfather.
wowFactor: Brando stuffed his cheeks with cotton for the audition — and won an Oscar for the role he almost didn't get.

quizQuestion: Which film is both a sequel AND prequel to The Godfather?
explanation: The Godfather Part II (1974) runs two parallel narratives: Michael Corleone's story in 1958 continuing from the first film, and young Vito Corleone's rise from Sicily to New York in the 1910s-20s.
statement: The Godfather Part II uniquely functions as both sequel and prequel to the original.
wowFactor: The Godfather Part II is the first sequel to win Best Picture — beating out the original film's own Oscar record.

quizQuestion: Which 1975 Spielberg film invented the summer blockbuster?
explanation: Jaws (1975), directed by Steven Spielberg, is widely credited with creating the modern summer blockbuster model, becoming the highest-grossing film of its time with a wide national release strategy.
statement: Jaws (1975) invented the summer blockbuster release model.
wowFactor: Jaws was the first film to earn over $100 million at the US box office — Hollywood has never been the same.

variants.question: Who directed Jaws (1975)?
Jaws (1975) was based on a novel by Peter Benchley. True or False?

quizQuestion: Who directed Jaws (1975)?
explanation: Steven Spielberg directed Jaws (1975) at age 27, battling a malfunctioning mechanical shark throughout the shoot. The shark's frequent failures forced Spielberg to imply its presence rather than show it — accidentally making the film scarier.
statement: Jaws was directed by Steven Spielberg.
wowFactor: The mechanical shark barely worked, so Spielberg hid it for most of the film — and accidentally made it far more terrifying.

variants.question: Which Spielberg film features police chief Brody hunting a great white shark?
Jaws was directed by Steven Spielberg. True or False?

quizQuestion: Who wrote and directed the original Star Wars (1977)?
explanation: George Lucas wrote and directed Star Wars (1977), creating the mythology of the Force, the Jedi, the Sith, and the Skywalker saga. He produced but did not direct any of the five sequel films.
statement: George Lucas wrote and directed the original Star Wars in 1977.
wowFactor: George Lucas was so convinced Star Wars would flop that he took a Hawaii vacation on opening day — only to return to a cultural phenomenon.

variants.question: Which director created the original Star Wars (1977)?
Star Wars (1977) was directed by George Lucas. True or False?

quizQuestion: Who wrote the screenplay for and starred in Rocky (1976)?
explanation: Sylvester Stallone wrote the Rocky screenplay and insisted on starring in the title role, despite studios wanting a bigger star. He wrote the script in three and a half days after being inspired by a Muhammad Ali fight.
statement: Sylvester Stallone wrote the Rocky screenplay and starred as the lead.
wowFactor: Stallone refused to sell his Rocky script unless he could star in it — studios offered him $300,000 to walk away, and he said no.

variants.question: Who played Rocky Balboa in the 1976 film Rocky?

quizQuestion: Which novella inspired Apocalypse Now (1979)?
explanation: Apocalypse Now (1979) is loosely based on Joseph Conrad's 1899 novella Heart of Darkness, transposing its story of a journey upriver to find a rogue figure from colonial Congo to the Vietnam War.
statement: Apocalypse Now was inspired by Joseph Conrad's novella Heart of Darkness.
wowFactor: Coppola moved Conrad's jungle nightmare from colonial Africa to Vietnam — and it worked just as devastatingly.

quizQuestion: Which Swiss artist designed the alien creature in Alien (1979)?
explanation: Swiss surrealist artist H. R. Giger designed the Xenomorph creature and sets for Alien (1979), drawing on his distinctive biomechanical art style. He won the Academy Award for Best Visual Effects for his work.
statement: Swiss artist H. R. Giger designed the alien creature in Ridley Scott's Alien.
wowFactor: H. R. Giger designed the Xenomorph to be 'perfectly terrifying' using biomechanical imagery — and it won him an Oscar.

quizQuestion: What car serves as the time machine in Back to the Future (1985)?
explanation: The DeLorean DMC-12 serves as the time machine in Back to the Future (1985). Robert Zemeckis chose it because the gull-wing doors looked futuristic, and the aluminum body made the time-travel light effects look better.

wowFactor: The DeLorean was chosen because its gull-wing doors looked alien and futuristic — ironically, the company went bankrupt the same year it became a pop culture icon.

quizQuestion: What visual style did Spielberg use for most of Schindler's List?
explanation: Steven Spielberg shot Schindler's List (1993) almost entirely in black and white, with one iconic exception: a little girl in a red coat is shown in color, symbolizing the individual lives lost in the Holocaust.
statement: Schindler's List was filmed in black and white, with a famous red coat as the sole color element.
wowFactor: Spielberg used black-and-white film in 1993 — and the one splash of red on a child's coat became one of cinema's most powerful moments.

quizQuestion: Which 1993 Spielberg film pioneered photo-realistic CGI dinosaurs?
explanation: Jurassic Park (1993) revolutionized visual effects by using computer-generated imagery (CGI) to create photo-realistic dinosaurs, combined with Stan Winston's animatronic creatures. It permanently shifted Hollywood toward CGI over practical effects.
statement: Jurassic Park (1993) pioneered photo-realistic CGI and changed Hollywood visual effects forever.
wowFactor: Jurassic Park's CGI team had only about six minutes of actual dinosaur footage — but it looked so real that Hollywood never went back.

statement: One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest was the second film ever to win all five major Academy Awards.

quizQuestion: What is the name of the murderous AI in 2001: A Space Odyssey?
explanation: HAL 9000 (Heuristically programmed ALgorithmic computer) is the artificial intelligence in Stanley Kubrick's 2001: A Space Odyssey (1968), voiced by Douglas Rain. HAL's calm refusal — 'I'm sorry Dave, I'm afraid I can't do that' — became one of cinema's most chilling lines.
statement: HAL 9000 is the murderous artificial intelligence in 2001: A Space Odyssey.
wowFactor: HAL's letters are each one step ahead of IBM in the alphabet — Kubrick denied it was intentional, but the coincidence haunts the film to this day.

quizQuestion: Which 1971 Kubrick film is based on Anthony Burgess's dystopian novel?
explanation: A Clockwork Orange (1971) was written, produced, and directed by Stanley Kubrick, based on Anthony Burgess's 1962 novel. Kubrick withdrew the film from UK distribution himself in 1974 after moral controversy, and it was not shown in Britain until after his death.
